112416.explanation: An employer has the responsibility to provide good housing for their employees, provided it is safe and livable.

112419.explanation: We want to encourage sustainability but also maintain conviviality. Looking for alternatives together.

112420.explanation: From 2026, it will be forbidden for residents to set off fireworks. To still celebrate the New Year festively, the municipality can facilitate a fireworks show in cooperation with local districts.

112423.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate. We support entrepreneurs by involving experts by experience to consider the accessibility of buildings and facilities in buildings for people with disabilities.

112424.explanation: In principle, we want to build within the cores, but customization may be necessary for farms.

112425.explanation: Local broadcasters, such as Twente FM and 1Twente, are important for the Twente identity and culture. We want to financially support regional broadcasters to ensure they can continue to exist.

112427.explanation: Culture and music enrich children. Where financial barriers exist, the municipality supports through the Leergeld Foundation.

112431.explanation: Dinkelland is currently one of the safest municipalities in the Netherlands. Therefore, more enforcement officers (boa's) are not needed to maintain safety.

112432.explanation: Dinkelland is currently one of the safest municipalities in the Netherlands. It is unnecessary to install more cameras. Residents should not be filmed unless it is necessary. They have a right to privacy.

112433.explanation: This is arranged by the central municipality of Enschede. It is the central facility within Twente.

112436.explanation: This can be allowed as a temporary solution.

112437.explanation: We are facing a large housing shortage. People find it very difficult to get a new home and have to wait a long time. If there is a good chance to build outside the existing village boundaries, the municipality should seriously consider it.

112438.explanation: This way people do something useful for society and become part of that society.

112439.explanation: Residents with low incomes deserve support. Debt forgiveness remains necessary.

112440.explanation: We consider this desirable, but national legislation makes this impossible.

112443.explanation: Safety for cyclists and pedestrians is paramount.

112444.explanation: The municipality has hardly any influence on the supply of healthcare providers.

112445.explanation: We want to keep taxes low, but maintain our facilities at a good level.

112447.explanation: The tourist tax contributes to our facilities, but tourism must remain affordable.

112448.explanation: We give space to entrepreneurs. In doing so, we pay attention to landscape, small scale, and local needs.

112454.explanation: We find it important that everyone learns to swim. The current swimming lesson system is sufficient. For children from low-income families there is funding available from the Leergeld Foundation.

112459.explanation: Residents in Dinkelland should be able to rent out a room if they wish to help solve the housing shortage. But only in their own home.

112460.explanation: More parking spaces must be created. This must not come at the expense of the quality of public spaces.

112464.explanation: A bypass provides more safety and reduces traffic pressure in the village.

112465.explanation: As long as the effects on health and living environment are unclear, we do not want wind turbines.

112466.explanation: There does not necessarily have to be a reception centre (azc) in Dinkelland. Dinkelland is dependent on national legislation for this. If the national government decides that an azc must be located here, we must comply. If it is not necessary, Lokaal Dinkelland does not want an azc.

112410.explanation: That is what the municipal council is elected for. However, residents should be involved earlier and better in decision-making so that their voice really counts.

112416.explanation: Housing must be good and safe, but does not always have to be on the owner's own plot. It must fit the location and be carefully integrated into the surroundings.

112419.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs should be able to choose for themselves, with attention to sustainability.

112420.explanation: We celebrate New Year's Eve in neighborhoods and together. A central fireworks show does not suit our city and villages.

112423.explanation: Accessibility is important, but this is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves.

112424.explanation: Living in rural areas must be carefully considered to protect the landscape and agricultural businesses.

112425.explanation: This is arranged regionally within the reform of the broadcasting system.

112427.explanation: Music education strengthens talent development and equal opportunities for all children.

112431.explanation: The municipality of Dinkelland is one of the safest municipalities in the country, so more enforcement officers are not immediately needed. However, the police are facing significant budget cuts. We continue to monitor where safety is at risk and respond appropriately.

112432.explanation: Not necessary, the municipality of Dinkelland is one of the safest in the Netherlands.

112433.explanation: This is arranged regionally, so a local facility is not necessary.

112436.explanation: The municipality must assess on a case-by-case basis whether this is possible. Allowing permanent residence, regardless of the situation, can also pose risks to the recreation sector and living environment.

112437.explanation: We build an additional neighborhood in each core so that young people, families, and seniors can find suitable housing, including in Agelo and Lattrop.

112438.explanation: Doing something back for society is logical, unless this is not possible.

112439.explanation: This remains a case-by-case matter. Some residents really need this support.

112440.explanation: The municipality should primarily support working people who just fall outside schemes. For this, CDA Dinkelland has arranged the customization budget.

112443.explanation: The speed limit is already 30 km/h in most streets within built-up areas. Lowering it further adds little.

112444.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but the municipality cannot and should not guarantee this.

112445.explanation: We keep the burdens for residents as low as possible.

112447.explanation: Tourists should contribute to the facilities they partly use, such as bike paths and museums.

112448.explanation: CDA Dinkelland has long advocated for more business plots to give local entrepreneurs space.

112454.explanation: Swimming lessons must be available for every child. Parents usually arrange this themselves, and families with lower incomes can receive support. If there is demand, we are willing to explore school swimming lessons.

112459.explanation: Room rental does not fit the character of our city and villages.

112460.explanation: There is sufficient parking space. The city or villages themselves indicate what is needed.

112464.explanation: There must be clarity about a bypass road. A bypass ensures safer traffic and less nuisance in Weerselo.

112465.explanation: This is provincial policy. By refusing agreements, control has been handed over to the province. We need to regain local control over the conditions of any wind turbines.

112466.explanation: We must comply with the law and therefore choose small-scale shelters that fit our municipality and facilities.

112410.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants to involve residents more actively in important decisions. An advisory referendum in the form of a citizens' panel provides valuable input. However, the municipal council remains ultimately responsible for the decision and makes the final choice.

112416.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants fair and safe housing for seasonal workers, with supervision and separate employment and rental contracts. This is to prevent abuses and exploitation. Existing housing is allowed on private business premises provided there are separate contracts and supervision.

112419.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants a strong local economy and attractive tourism. Banning terrace heaters harms our entrepreneurs and hospitality. Entrepreneurs should decide for themselves whether to use terrace heaters.

112420.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD does not see this as a municipal task. It also costs a lot of public money. We choose to strengthen our own traditions by keeping carbidschieten safe and responsible via the BOCK campaign. We want to explore possibilities to adjust the General Local Regulation (APV).

112423.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD is against municipal subsidies for accessibility of business premises. This is not a core task and increases the burden for all residents. We choose healthy finances and encourage entrepreneurs to take responsibility themselves. The municipality can advise in this.

112424.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants farmers to be able to build houses on and around their farms themselves, provided this fits well into the landscape. Clustered farmyards are beautiful examples that fit into our scenic landscape and enhance the livability of the countryside.

112425.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD finds additional municipal support for the local broadcaster unnecessary. The broadcaster should ensure a healthy operation through income from the Municipal Fund and advertising so that local taxes do not have to be unnecessarily increased.

112427.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD is against municipal subsidies for music lessons. Primary schools can allocate funds themselves if they consider it important.

112431.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants a safe municipality but not more enforcement officers (boa’s). We choose targeted deployment of existing officers, cooperation with police, and prevention. Additional enforcement officers increase costs, while Dinkelland already ranks among the safest municipalities in the Netherlands.

112432.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD is currently cautious about more camera surveillance. We choose cooperation with police, boa’s, and residents. For now, we prefer to invest in prevention and social safety.

112433.explanation: If there is a need in Twente for an (additional) women's shelter, the Dinkelland VVD believes this should be organized regionally within the 14 Twente municipalities. Cooperation ensures quality, efficiency, and lower costs without each municipality investing separately.

112436.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD opposes permanent residence in holiday homes. We want holiday parks to remain holiday parks. This preserves our tourist and agricultural identity and prevents pressure on facilities and the living environment.

112437.explanation: We must build, build, build. The Dinkelland VVD prefers that all available space within the centers is built up first. But infill alone will not suffice. Therefore, new construction must also be allowed outside the centers.

112438.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants everyone to participate. People with social assistance benefits should do socially meaningful work wherever possible. This increases self-worth, strengthens social bonds, and can lead faster to a paid job.

112439.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants to stop waiving municipal taxes. Everyone contributes according to their ability. We choose targeted support through minimum income policy and work participation, not generic exemptions that shift burdens.

112440.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD opposes raising the income limit for poverty assistance. We choose targeted support for those who really need it. Additionally, we prefer to stimulate self-reliance and work so residents improve rather than remain dependent.

112443.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants to introduce 30 km/h zones where this demonstrably contributes to safety and learnability, such as near schools, residential areas, and crossings. One example is Bisschopstraat in Weerselo, which will partly be designated as a 30 km/h area.

112444.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD finds freedom of choice in healthcare important. But if this leads to extra costs, for example because the preferred care provider must come from the Randstad to Dinkelland, then those costs should be borne personally.

112445.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD does not want to unnecessarily increase the property tax (ozb) and aims to reduce it where possible. Low burdens keep housing affordable and strengthen the business climate. We focus on efficient spending and financial responsibility to ensure any reduction is financially sustainable.

112447.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD does not want to increase the tourism tax. This tax is intended to support tourism, not to cover budget shortfalls. Higher burdens harm entrepreneurs and visitors and undermine our hospitable municipality.

112448.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants more space for entrepreneurs. New industrial sites in Denekamp, Ootmarsum, and Weerselo are needed to stimulate a strong economy and thereby safeguard livability in the centers.

112454.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD values school swimming for safety and health but finds structural financing unfeasible. Parents take on this responsibility. Additionally, there are options in the municipality for parents who cannot afford their children's swimming lessons.

112459.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants creative solutions for the housing shortage. We support splitting houses and using existing buildings for housing provided these lead to independent homes and contribute to flow and affordability.

112460.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD wants centers to remain accessible and livable. Therefore, we focus on sufficient parking at new developments, but also for current residents and future visitors, with clear signage and good parking policy, especially in busy centers like Ootmarsum.

112464.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD has always expressed doubts about the usefulness and necessity of the bypass but accepts the democratically made decision in the municipal council and Provincial States that the bypass must be built.

112465.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD rejects windmills due to their major impact on our landscape and living environment. We choose sustainable energy sources such as geothermal energy, manure digesters, and, in cooperation with Twente, small modular reactors, not large-scale wind turbines without local support.

112466.explanation: The Dinkelland VVD is against an asylum seekers center (azc) in Dinkelland. There are already reception facilities in Northeast Twente (on the edge of Denekamp and in Tubbergen) to which Dinkelland municipality also contributes. Moreover, the dispersion law is currently under debate in The Hague.

112410.explanation: Many topics are not suitable for a referendum; listening well to residents and then making a balanced decision is wiser. People cannot always see the bigger picture; we as the council can.

112416.explanation: This way, as an entrepreneur, you take responsibility for your own staff. You also prevent problems such as nuisance in neighborhoods and districts. It is important that when someone leaves, they still have some time to find another option.

112419.explanation: It costs a lot of energy, while one can also stay inside or put on a blanket. Sitting outside in winter or autumn is not necessary anyway.

112420.explanation: Is very expensive and is not a task for the municipality. A possible subsidy could be considered, especially if it contributes to the welfare of the residents.

112423.explanation: If necessary, the municipality may assist with this. It is important that everyone can participate in society. Good accessibility is part of that.

112424.explanation: This would lead to uncontrolled development in the rural area, which we should actually cherish. A lot is already possible within the rules.

112425.explanation: If people need it and can demonstrate added value, then fine. Otherwise not.

112427.explanation: It is important to discover where your aptitude/talent lies. Music lessons are quite expensive; offering them at a reduced rate can help to still give it a try. Money should not be a barrier to trying something. The same applies to membership of a sports club.

112431.explanation: If this should be necessary, then it must be done.

112432.explanation: If this unfortunately should be necessary, then it must be done. But only for major events; we must be cautious not to record everything on film.

112433.explanation: Domestic violence also occurs in Dinkelland; therefore, it is good that there are facilities in Dinkelland where people can find peace if they are in this situation.

112436.explanation: That is not a solution to the housing crisis; more construction is needed. It may help if this is temporarily allowed, but not permanently.

112437.explanation: New construction outside existing village boundaries should be possible, provided it fits with that boundary, thereby effectively shifting the boundary. If this is appropriate, it should be allowed.

112438.explanation: Not everyone chooses to receive social assistance benefits; sometimes people simply cannot help it. To then obligate these people goes too far. If they want to do that, fine, but mandating it goes too far.

112439.explanation: There are always residents who need to use this because of unforeseen circumstances to make ends meet. That must remain possible. It also reflects a degree of solidarity within society for fellow residents.

112440.explanation: Given the current times with rising prices, it is becoming increasingly difficult to make ends meet. By raising the income ceiling to 130 percent of the minimum wage, more people qualify to participate in intended schemes. There is funding for that, so fine.

112443.explanation: This contributes to safety within the villages.

112444.explanation: It is important that people feel safe when they need help. They often find that among people with similar beliefs. If these meet the set requirements, that is fine.

112445.explanation: Property tax (OZB) is used to finance various matters that are in the interest of the entire municipality. It is therefore important that everyone contributes their part.

112447.explanation: Tourists fortunately know how to find the municipality well; the municipality invests in good facilities for these tourists such as cycle paths, museums, and hospitality. It is fair for them to contribute to this.

112448.explanation: We already have enough industrial estates. We see much more potential in revitalizing existing sites and redeveloping them if desired. There is no room for large companies in any case.

112454.explanation: It is very important that every child learns to swim; this should not depend on whether there is money at home. Teaching this at school prevents anyone from being unable to save themselves if they unexpectedly end up in the water.

112459.explanation: This could lead to a potential uncontrolled growth of landlords. There are other ways to meet the need. For example, homes can be split. Temporarily renting a room to, for example, a divorced person should be possible, especially if they have no other solution.

112460.explanation: It is better to invest in good cycling connections and good public transport links. Then one does not need a car to visit a village but can simply take the bus or bike. Public transport must be affordable; work still needs to be done on that.

112464.explanation: It has now been proven that the ring road is no longer necessary; due to various other developments over the years, the need has decreased. The money can be better used to adapt the current situation. Additional asphalt at the expense of nature should be prevented if possible.

112465.explanation: To meet the goal of being energy neutral by 2050, we cannot avoid installing wind turbines. We must ensure they are well integrated and that society benefits from them. However, we must monitor health risks and act if necessary.

112466.explanation: This is a legal task that all municipalities have been assigned under the distribution law, obliging us to establish an asylum seekers' center (AZC) in Dinkelland. From a solidarity perspective, we also find it important to contribute our part as Dinkelland.

112410.explanation: BBB-Dinkelland supports more input from the municipality's residents through conversations, information meetings, and surveys. In a referendum, the conditions must be clearly described; the complexity often means citizens cannot form a good judgment and make emotional choices.

112416.explanation: Employers must provide good accommodation for seasonal workers on their own premises or near the location.

112419.explanation: BBB-Dinkelland believes this is up to the local entrepreneur themselves.

112420.explanation: Traditions such as shooting carbide should be preserved. A joint fireworks show near the villages could be an alternative if a fireworks ban comes into effect.

112423.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for the accessibility of their buildings, including for people with disabilities.

112424.explanation: There are possibilities such as the current red-for-red schemes where houses can be built on private property. We encourage tailored solutions where necessary.

112425.explanation: We support the local broadcasters but currently do not think extra money is needed for them. BBB Dinkelland believes that money can be better spent on things like safety.

112427.explanation: We leave this up to the primary school itself.

112431.explanation: This is to support the police and to be able to guarantee the safety of our residents.

112432.explanation: It is wise to have more camera surveillance in problem areas.

112433.explanation: BBB-Dinkelland finds it important that there must be a safe place for all residents, young or old, if they experience some form of threat, abuse, or stalking.

112436.explanation: This is a temporary solution to address this urgent problem.

112437.explanation: Both young and old must have the opportunity to build in the rural area where this is necessary.

112438.explanation: BBB-Dinkelland believes that contributions should be made according to one's abilities.

112439.explanation: We look after our residents; we are in favor of tailored solutions where necessary and possible.

112440.explanation: This depends on the financial condition of the municipality of Dinkelland.

112443.explanation: This must be assessed on the spot per situation.

112444.explanation: If possible, take it into account, but we find receiving care more important.

112445.explanation: If the finances of the municipality of Dinkelland allow, we are in favor of lowering the property tax (ozb).

112447.explanation: An increase has already been implemented recently.

112448.explanation: Develop according to the needs of the local entrepreneurs.

112454.explanation: We think it is important that every child can learn to swim.

112459.explanation: This is a good solution to do something about the housing crisis.

112460.explanation: For example, in Ootmarsum this is a big problem. We want to do something about that by organizing things differently.

112464.explanation: There are still enough adjustments that can be made in the current process so that a bypass is not necessary at this moment.

112465.explanation: Large wind turbines do not fit in our local landscape and the power grid is not ready for them here.

112466.explanation: BBB Dinkelland is against the establishment of an asylum seekers' center (AZC) in Dinkelland.

statements.title: A ring road around Weerselo should be built.
Residents of Dinkelland should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Dinkelland moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
Dinkelland should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: There should be an asylum seekers' centre in Dinkelland.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Dinkelland should have a women's shelter.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Dinkelland.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: There should be more business parks in Dinkelland.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Wind turbines should be built near Denekamp.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
